content:
Hi,

These limits are set and enforced by server-side mailing software, so as a user you can't change them. If you don't want to use other methods of sharing, you might send the files in separate mails.

Or if the images are unnecessarily large, you should consider downscaling them, either using an image editor, or you could also use an extension for this. I've found these: Inline Image Resizer and Easy Image Compressor. The former only seems to work for in-line images, the latter also works with attachments.

Regards, Balázs
creator: meskobalazs

content: You may also want to take a look at the File Link feature. https://support.mozilla.org/kb/filelink-large-attachments

content: The certificate has been issued for stackmail.com. However, with Thunderbird you're connecting to the server imap.peternedsmith.co.uk. There is a mismatch, hence you do get the exception prompt. Check with your email/hosting provider about the correct email server settings for your domain, or have them fix their certificate.

content:
I guess this is also a silly reply. They are what happens when folks post without any relevant information. Try the following:

  • Restart Thunderbird with add-ons disabled (Thunderbird Safe Mode). On the Help menu, click on "Restart with Add-ons Disabled". If Thunderbird works like normal, there is an Add-on or Theme interfering with normal operations. You will need to re-enable add-ons one at a time until you locate the offender.
  • Restart the operating system in safe mode with Networking. This loads only the very basics needed to start your computer while enabling an Internet connection. Click on your operating system for instructions on how to start in safe mode: Windows 10, Windows 8, Windows 7, Windows Vista, Windows XP, OSX
If safe mode for the operating system fixes the issue, there's other software in your computer that's causing problems. Possibilities include but not limited to: AV scanning, virus/malware, background downloads such as program updates.
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
Try this:

- go to TB menu > Settings > General

- scroll all the way down and click the 'Config editor' button on the right

- click 'Accept the risk and continue' if that appears

- search for mailnews.oauth.usePrivateBrowser > change the value to true

- restart TB
